title: HTML Link Semantics, Base URLs, Relations, and Discovery Decisions · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for html link semantics, base urls, relations, and discovery decisions and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for semantic link-discovery ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Enumerate link-bearing HTML elements and distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, form, media, and embedded destinations.
Apply first-valid-base rules against the final response URL and retain base source and rejected alternatives.
Interpret rel tokens, download, target, media, hreflang, canonical, alternate, and nofollow under an explicit discovery policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
